update: q12 - actualy, the too nice template is for when they send you a proeprty that is too nice, maybe its already rehabbed, or doesnt need a full remodel. for whatever reason, it is "too nice" to make an investment deal or be sold by a wholesaler. fix this question and answer - and add a new question to the pool as well

"Float the deal first" has the wrong answer in it. After they say "let me ask the seller," you should wait for a response from them (with consistent and daily follow up) until you get the price you need, or move on. 
Also, Pool Q16. How should you respond if a seller pushes back on a $1 option fee? Has the incorrect answer.
q25 - always call/contact whoever is closest to the money first. offer out, terms sent, clear inbox, then your follow up lists

q24 - we tell the realtor we need to get the contractors in - then explain how that works - but what is actualy happenign on our end is that we are waiting to send our buyers in after we have it in contract and hopefuly get a deal sold
